items.title: Trump on immigration crackdown: 'We have no apologies'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump regals House Republicans with a rhetorical highlight reel from his first week in office, and urges them to support his immigration crackdown and border security proposals. “We have no apologies and we’re moving very fast," he said. 

#trump #news #immigration #donaldtrump 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Scott Bessent confirmed as Trump's treasury secretary
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The U.S. Senate confirmed billionaire investor Scott Bessent on Monday to serve as President Donald Trump's treasury secretary, giving him the delicate balancing act of cutting taxes and curbing deficits while putting forward a plan on tariffs that doesn't jeopardize growth. Read more: https://bit.ly/4gbiU17 

#scottbessent #trump #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: What to know about the U.S. showdown with Colombia over deported migrants
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The White House claimed victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S. on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Israel allows Palestinians to return to most heavily destroyed part of Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israel has begun allowing Palestinians to return to the heavily destroyed north of the Gaza Strip for the first time since the early weeks of the 15-month war with Hamas. That's in accordance with a fragile ceasefire. Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed north Monday after waiting for days to cross. Read more: https://bit.ly/4gdwXmK

items.title: South Korea President Yoon indicted on rebellion charges
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has become the country's first leader to be indicted, less than two weeks after he was the first to be detained.

The impeached, jailed president, who had been holed up in his presidential compound for weeks after issuing a shocking martial law decree last month, now faces rebellion charges that are punishable by the death penalty or life in prison. Read more: https://bit.ly/4avRnGl 

#southkorea #news 
Photo credit: Pool EPA

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: LIVE: UN Holocaust Memorial marks 80th anniversary of Auschwitz liberation
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Holocaust Memorial Ceremony at the United Nations marks the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz on Jan. 27, 1945.

The ceremony is hosted by Melissa Fleming, the United Nations Under-Secretary-General for Global Communications. Holocaust survivors are expected to share their testimonies

#holocaustmemorial #auschwitzbirkenau #unitednations #news #live</article>

items.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A portion of the Pacific Coast Highway in Los Angeles County was closed on Sunday afternoon due to mudflows as rain fell in parts of Southern California. The scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Read more: https://bit.ly/3E9gToB

#wildfire #california #usa #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Haitian migrants in Ohio turn to faith amid deportation fears
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Despite legally migrating to the U.S. under the country’s Temporary Protected Status program, some Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, are fearful President Donald Trump’s promised migration crackdown will soon include them. Many are searching for solace in their faith. (AP Video/Jessie Wardarski). Read more: https://bit.ly/4h9Z5bK

#immigration #haiti #usa #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Dubai’s boom is putting strains on its residents, with traffic one of top worries
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Skyscraper-studded Dubai has been on a hot streak for the last five years, But all that growth comes with strains for the city’s population. Traffic feels worse than ever on Dubai’s roads and the price of housing continues to spike even with new real estate projects being announced almost daily. 
(AP video shot by: Malak Harb).

#dubai #news

items.content:

Zelenskyy, together with representatives of the Jewish community and Ukrainian officials, held a ceremony at the memorial in the capital where they paid their respects to the victims of the Holocaust.

items.title: Residents of kibbutz destroyed in Israel-Hezbollah fighting worry about safety on return
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The proximity to the Lebanese border made Manara so vulnerable in the war between Israel and Hezbollah that it became a symbol of the heavy price of fighting, with rockets and explosive drones damaging the majority of homes. The kibbutz was evacuated along with some 60,000 people, who the Israeli government told to leave dozens of towns and villages along the border. (AP video shot by Ami Ben Tov) Read more here: https://bit.ly/42rxgHd

#israel #lebanon #news

Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Planetary parade 2025 LIVE: Watch alignment of six planets in the night sky
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live telescope views of six planets gracing the sky in what’s known as a planetary parade. Venus, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn are visible to the naked eye this month and for part of February. Uranus and Neptune can be spotted with binoculars and telescopes. Read more here: https://bit.ly/40zxXvP
Credit: Gianluca Masi-Virtual Telescope Project

#space #planet #live</article>

items.title: Thousands gather in Wadi Gaza, hoping to cross to the north of the Strip
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinian families have been displaced from the northern Gaza Strip since October 2023 when the Israel-Hamas war erupted. According to the ceasefire deal, the Israeli military had been expected to allow hundreds of thousands of Palestinian to return to northern Gaza through a road that bisects the territory starting Sunday.

items.content:

items.content:

items.title: India's Republic Day Parade 2025: Watch the full event in New Delhi
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>India celebrates Republic Day with a parade in New Delhi showcasing the country’s defense capability and cultural diversity. The event marks the anniversary of the adoption of the country’s Constitution on Jan. 26, 1950, nearly three years after it won independence from British colonial rule. Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto will be the chief guest at the parade.

#india #republicday #live</article>

items.title: Trump leans into his pledge to eliminate taxes on tips at a Las Vegas rally
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: The destroyed hospitals in the Gaza Strip
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinians in Gaza are confronting an apocalyptic landscape of devastation after more than 15 months of fighting between Israel and Hamas. Mounds of rubble stretch as far as the eye can see in the tiny coastal enclave where built-up refugee camps are interspersed between cities. Gaza's hospitals lie in ruins, leaving northern Gaza with almost no functional health services.

#gaza #palestinian #news

items.content:
